name: The Watcher Moves Toward Home
description: Scholar Maren has been tracking two star charts from the same window that don't match. The Watcher constellation is moving. She needs someone to take the charts to Pilot Kira, who has Fleet data on the same pattern.
accept_text: She is in the upper chamber when you find her, sitting on the floor below the telescope with both charts spread in front of her — not at the lens, on the floor, the way of someone who has been looking at the same thing for a very long time and needed to change the angle. She stands when she sees you. 'Maren,' she says. 'Aetherian High Council, crystal research attachment, seconded to the monastery for the lens.' She gestures at the charts. 'I've been tracking the Watcher constellation for eight months. Those two charts represent what I've observed and what the instruments show. They don't match.' She points to the differences with the precision of someone who has been pointing at these same differences for weeks and is very tired of being the only person in the room when she does it. 'Those stars should be fixed. They are not. They are moving in a consistent, directional pattern.' She rolls the newer chart carefully and holds it out. 'Pilot Kira of the Skyborn Fleet has been mapping the lattice pattern below the Wastes from altitude for eight months. The Fleet has also been tracking this constellation — I saw a Fleet intelligence summary three months ago that named it.' She pauses. 'I need you to take this to her. If the movement pattern in the sky and the pattern she's mapping in the lattice correspond — if they match — that is the Elder People building something to last across three hundred years of war.' She looks at the chart in your hands. 'The Fleet summary I read named the constellation using the Elder sky-map designation. Do you know what that name means?' She asks it the way academics ask questions they already know the answer to but want you to arrive at yourself. 'The Elder People named it the Watcher. A fixed point. Other measurements were taken against it. Fixed references do not move.' She looks at the chart. 'And yet.'
complete_text: Kira unrolls the chart with the care of someone handling navigational equipment — precise, no wasted motion. She reads it not as an image but as a sequence: a path, a direction, a destination. She is quiet for a long time. 'The Fleet has been tracking this for two years,' she says. Not surprised. The tone of someone whose hypothesis has been confirmed and who is processing whether that's a relief or a new problem. She pulls her own log from the navigation panel. 'The Skyborn navigational tradition preserves the Elder sky-maps alongside our own. When the Watcher started moving eight months ago the Fleet looked up the pattern and found it named.' She shows you the Elder notation beside the Fleet record. 'Fixed references don't move. That's the whole reason you use them — they give you something absolute to measure against.' She looks at Maren's chart, then at the Fleet log. 'The position data matches. What Maren has been observing from the ground matches what the Fleet has been tracking from above.' She is quiet. 'Two months ago I found a note in the Elder sky-maps beside the Watcher entry. A conditional note — the Elder People annotated their navigational constants with usage conditions.' She finds it: a small notation, Elder navigational script, beside the Watcher's coordinates. 'It says: when the work is complete, the Watcher returns to its home position.' She looks at you. 'The Watcher is not moving randomly. It is moving toward a specific destination — toward wherever it was before the work began. It is moving home.' She is quiet with the particular quiet of a person standing at the edge of something very large. 'I've been flying above this war for fifteen years. I have been watching the Gray Wastes from altitude for fifteen years.' She looks at the chart. 'The war is not what's happening. The war is what's been happening while the actual thing completes.' She rolls both charts together — Maren's and the Fleet log — and puts them in her navigation case. 'Tell Maren: the Fleet confirms it. Tell her: the Watcher is going home.'
